title: Virotia leptophylla
intro: There is no information available on a plant called "Virotia leptophylla".
taxonomy: Virotia leptophylla is a plant species classified in the family Santalaceae, which is a group of parasitic plants commonly known as sandalwoods or mistletoes. The family Santalaceae belongs to the order Santalales, which consists of parasitic flowering plants. Santalales is a part of the larger clade Santalanae, within the angiosperm clade of flowering plants. This classification places Virotia leptophylla within the broader group of flowering plants, specifically the eudicots, which are one of the most diverse groups of flowering plants. Overall, the classification of Virotia leptophylla reflects its unique parasitic nature and evolutionary history within the plant kingdom.
